channel.title: summarizedStoriesHealthOffshootNL
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from summarizedStoriesHealthOffshootNL, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Suicide rates have declined since the launch of 988 suicide hotline, study finds
item.description: Suicide rates among teens and young adults declined in the two years after the launch of the 988 Suicide and Crisis Lifeline.

item.title: ‘What the Health?’ Podcast: RFK Jr. vs. Congress
item.description: HHS Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. completed his tour of House and Senate committees this week, ostensibly to promote President Donald Trump’s budget...

item.title: Readers Chime In on Reproductive Rights, Therapy Chatbots, Medical Debt, and More
item.description: KFF Health News gives readers a chance to comment on a recent batch of stories.

item.title: ‘What the Health?’ Podcast: RFK Jr. vs. Congress
item.description: HHS Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. completed his tour of House and Senate committees this week, ostensibly to promote President Donald Trump’s budget...

item.title: A ‘Barbaric’ Problem in American Hospitals Is Only Getting Bigger
item.description: Patients are getting stuck in the emergency department for days while waiting for a spot in an inpatient ward.

item.title: Readers Chime In on Reproductive Rights, Therapy Chatbots, Medical Debt, and More
item.description: KFF Health News gives readers a chance to comment on a recent batch of stories.

item.title: The FDA gives the green light to the first gene therapy for deafness
item.description: The treatment, developed by Regeneron Pharmaceuticals, is for a very rare form of deafness. But it represents a medical milestone.

item.title: Trump administration eases rules on some marijuana categories. Here's what to know
item.description: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche said he is immediately moving medical marijuana from Schedule I to Schedule III, which includes drugs like ketami...

item.title: Two startlingly different views on long-awaited data on America's anti-HIV efforts
item.description: After a year without data, the State Department released figures on PEPFAR, the program launched by George W. Bush and credited with saving millions o...

item.title: How mosquitoes — and malaria — helped shape the whereabouts of early humankind
item.description: A new study looks at an unexpected force that played a critical role in shaping the lives of ancient humans.

item.title: Adrian Owen & Faraz Shafaghi, Creyos
item.description: I spoke with neuroscientist Adrian Owen, co-founder and Chief Scientific Officer & Faraz Shafaghi, Chief Product Officer, at neurological testing comp...

item.title: Dayne Williams, Quantum Health
item.description: Quantum Health is one of the biggest and original navigation companies. Dayne Williams came out of retirement to take over as CEO after Zane Burke ret...

item.title: Philippe Pouletty, Carvolix
item.description: Philippe Pouletty is a physician who’s an inventor, French venture capitalist, and the founder of Carvolix. Carvolix is a medical technology company t...

item.title: Peter Stetson, TigerConnect
item.description: Peter Stetson is the CMIO of TigerConnect. It’s now calling itself an AI platform to connect people, especially workflows between doctors, nurses, EMT...

item.title: A Unified Sense of Self
item.description: By MIKE MAGEE Stanford neuroscientist, David Eagleman, reminded us this week that “A coherent explanation of consciousness eludes modern science.” Tha...
